volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 1.3.216b - Section 2.2.302)

sections.label: Section 1.3.216b
sections.original: Ego hercle vero te et servabo et te sequar ,
sections.translation: By my troth , I certainly shall keep an eye on you , and follow you . I wouldn ’ t take the wealth of the Gods to lose you this day .

sections.label: Section 1.3.218
sections.original: Evocate intus Culindrum mihi coquom actutum foras .
sections.translation: Call Cylindrus , the cook , out of doors this moment from within .

sections.label: Section 1.4.219
sections.original: sportulam cape atque argentum . eccos tris nummos habes .
sections.translation: Take a hand-basket and some money . See , you have three didrachmns here .

sections.label: Section 1.4.220
sections.original: Habeo .
sections.translation: I have so .

sections.label: Section 1.4.220b
sections.original: Abi atque obsonium adfer ; tribus vide quod sit satis :
sections.translation: Go and bring some provisions , see that there ’ s enough for three ; let it be neither deficient nor overmuch .

sections.label: Section 1.4.221b
sections.original: Cuius modi hí homines erunt ?
sections.translation: What sort of persons are these to be ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.222
sections.original: Ego et Menaechmus et parasitus eius .
sections.translation: Myself , Menaechmus , and his Parasite .

sections.label: Section 1.4.222b
sections.original: Iam isti sunt decem ;
sections.translation: Then these make ten , for the Parasite easily performs the duty of eight persons [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.4.224
sections.original: Elocuta sum convivas , ceterum cura .
sections.translation: I ’ ve now told you the guests ; do you take care of the rest .

sections.label: Section 1.4.224b
sections.original: Licet .
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 1.4.225
sections.original: cocta sunt , iube ire accubitum .
sections.translation: It ’ s cooked already ; bid them go and take their places .

sections.label: Section 1.4.225b
sections.original: Redi cito .
sections.translation: Make haste back .

sections.label: Section 1.4.225c
sections.original: Iam ego hic ero .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll be here directly .

sections.label: Section 2.1.226
sections.original: Voluptas nullast navitis , Messenio ,
sections.translation: There ’ s no greater pleasure to voyagers , in my notion , Messenio , than at the moment when from sea they espy the land afar .

sections.label: Section 2.1.228b
sections.original: Maior , non dicam dolo ,
sections.translation: There is a greater , I ’ ll say it without subterfuge ,— if on your arrival you see the land that is your own .

sections.label: Section 2.1.230
sections.original: sed quaeso , quam ob rem nunc Epidamnum venimus ?
sections.translation: But , prithee , why are we now come to Epidamnus ? Why , like the sea , are we going round all the islands ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.232
sections.original: Fratrem quaesitum geminum germanum meum .
sections.translation: To seek for my own twin-brother born ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.233
sections.original: Nam quid modi futurum est illum quaerere ?
sections.translation: Why , what end is there to be of searching for him ? This is the sixth year that we ’ ve devoted our attention to this business .

sections.label: Section 2.1.235
sections.original: Histros , Hispanos , Massiliensis , Hilurios ,
sections.translation: We have been already carried round the Istrians [*] , the Hispanians , the Massilians , the Illyrians , all the Upper Adriatic Sea , and foreign Greece [*] , and all the shores of Italy , wherever the sea reaches them . If you had been searching for a needle , I do believe you would , long ere this , have found the needle , if it were visible .

sections.label: Section 2.1.240
sections.original: hominem inter vivos quaeritamus mortuom ;
sections.translation: Among the living are we seeking a person that ’ s dead ; for long ago should we have found him if he had been alive .

sections.label: Section 2.1.242
sections.original: Ergo istuc quaero certum qui faciat mihi ,
sections.translation: For that reason I am looking for a person to give me that information for certain , who can say that he knows that he really is dead ; after that I shall never take any trouble in seeking further .

sections.label: Section 2.1.245
sections.original: verum aliter vivos numquam desistam exsequi .
sections.translation: But otherwise I shall never , while I ’ m alive , desist ; I know how dear he is to my heart .

sections.label: Section 2.1.247
sections.original: In scirpo nodum quaeris . quin nos hinc domum
sections.translation: You are seeking a knot in a bulrush [*] . Why don ’ t we return homeward hence , unless we are to write a history [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.249
sections.original: Dictum facessas , datum edis , caveas malo .
sections.translation: Have done with your witty sayings , and be on your guard against a mischief .

sections.label: Section 2.1.250
sections.original: molestus ne sis , non tuo hoc fiet modo .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be troublesome ; this matter shan ’ t be done at your bidding .

sections.label: Section 2.1.250b
sections.original: Em
sections.translation: Aye , aye ; by that same expression do I rest assured that I ’ m a slave ; he couldn ’ t in a few words have said more in a plain-spoken way . But still I can ’ t restrain myself from speaking . ( Aloud .) Do you hear , Menaechmus ? When I look in the purse ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.255
sections.original: viaticati hercle admodum aestive sumus .
sections.translation: I find , i ’ faith , we ’ re only equipped for our journey like summer travellers [*] . By my troth , I guess , if you don ’ t be returning home , while you ’ re seeking your twin-brother , you ’ ll surely be groaning [*] , when you have nothing left . For such is this race of people ; among the men of Epidamnus there are debauchees and very great drinkers ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.260
sections.original: tum sycophantae et palpatores plurumi
sections.translation: swindlers besides , and many wheedlers are living in this city ; then the women in the harlot line are said nowhere in the world to be more captivating . The name of Epidamnus was given to this city for the very reason , because hardly any person sojourns here without some damnable mishaps [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.1.265
sections.original: Ego istúc cavebo . cedo dum huc mihi marsuppium .
sections.translation: I ’ ll guard against that . Just give me the purse this way .

sections.label: Section 2.1.266
sections.original: Quid eo vis ?
sections.translation: What do you want with it ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.266b
sections.original: Iam aps te metuo de verbis tuis .
sections.translation: I ’ m apprehensive then about yourself , from your expressions .

sections.label: Section 2.1.267
sections.original: Quid metuis ?
sections.translation: Why are you apprehensive ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.267b
sections.original: Ne mihi damnum in Epidamno duis .
sections.translation: Lest you should cause me some damnable mishap in Epidamnus . You are a great admirer of the women , Messenio , and I ’ m a passionate man , of an unmanageable disposition ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.270
sections.original: id utrumque , argentum quando habebo , cavero ,
sections.translation: of both these things will I have a care , when I ’ ve got the money , that you shall not commit a fault , and that I shall not be in a passion with you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.272
sections.original: Cape atque serva . me lubente feceris .
sections.translation: Take and keep it ; with all my heart you may do so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.273
sections.original: Bene opsonavi atque ex mea sententia ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve catered well , and to my mind . I ’ ll set a good breakfast before the breakfasters .

sections.label: Section 2.2.275
sections.original: sed eccúm Menaechmum video . vae tergo meo ,
sections.translation: But see , I perceive Menaechmus . Woe to my back ; the guests are now already walking before the door , before I ’ ve returned with the provisions . I ’ ll go and accost him . Save you , Menaechmus .

sections.label: Section 2.2.278b
sections.original: Di te amabunt quisquis es .
sections.translation: The Gods bless you , whoever you are . ⋯

sections.label: Section 2.2.279
sections.original: Quisquis ⋯ quis ego sim ?
sections.translation: ⋯ who I am ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.280
sections.original: Non hercle vero .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , not I , indeed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.280b
sections.original: Vbi convivae ceteri ?
sections.translation: Where are the other guests ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.281
sections.original: Quos tu convivas quaeris ?
sections.translation: What guests are you enquiring about ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.281b
sections.original: Parasitum tuom .
sections.translation: Your Parasite .

sections.label: Section 2.2.282
sections.original: Meum parasitum ? certe hic insanust homo .
sections.translation: My Parasite ? Surely this fellow ’ s deranged .

sections.label: Section 2.2.283
sections.original: Dixin tibi esse hic sycophantas plurumos ?
sections.translation: Didn ’ t I tell you that there were many swindlers here ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.285
sections.original: Quem tu parasitum quaeris , adulescens , meum ?
sections.translation: What Parasite of mine , young man , are you enquiring about ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.286
sections.original: Peniculum éccum in vidulo salvom fero .
sections.translation: See , I ’ ve got your sponge [*] [ Peniculus ] all safe in the wallet .

sections.label: Section 2.2.287
sections.original: Menaechme , numero huc advenis ad prandium .
sections.translation: Menaechmus , you ’ ve come here too soon for breakfast ; I ’ m but now returning with the provisions .

sections.label: Section 2.2.288b
sections.original: Responde mihi ,
sections.translation: Answer me this , young man : at what price do pigs sell here [*] ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.290
sections.original: sacres sinceri ?
sections.translation: unblemished ones , for sacrifice ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.290b
sections.original: Nummis .
sections.translation: At a didrachm a-piece .

sections.label: Section 2.2.290c
sections.original: Nummum a me accipe :
sections.translation: Receive , then , a didrachm of me ; bid a sacrifice be made for you at my expense ; for , by my faith , I really am sure in very truth that you are deranged , who are annoying me , a person that ’ s a stranger , whoever you are .

sections.label: Section 2.2.294
sections.original: Cylindrus ego sum : non nosti nomen meum ?
sections.translation: I am Cylindrus ; don ’ t you know my name ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.295
sections.original: Si tu Cylindrus seu Coriendrus , perieris .
sections.translation: Whether you are Cylindrus or Caliendrus [*] , confound you . I don ’ t know you , and , in fact , I don ’ t want to know you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.297
sections.original: Est tibi Menaechmo nomen , tantum quod sciam .
sections.translation: Well , your name , however , is Menaechmus , that I do know .

sections.label: Section 2.2.298
sections.original: Pro sano loqueris quom me appellas nomine .
sections.translation: You speak like a sane person when you call me by my name . But where have you known me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.299b
sections.original: Vbi ego te noverim ,
sections.translation: Where have I known you ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.300
sections.original: qui amicam habes eram meam hanc Erotium ?
sections.translation: you who have Erotium , this mistress of mine ( pointing to the house ) , for your lady ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.301
sections.original: Neque hercle ego habeo , neque te quis homo sis scio .
sections.translation: By my troth , I have not , nor do I know yourself what person you are .

sections.label: Section 2.2.302
sections.original: Non scis quis ego sim , qui tibi saepissime
sections.translation: Not know who I am , who have many a time filled the cups for your own self at our house , when you ’ ve been drinking ?
